explanation: The command 'net view' is a direct match for the Sigma rule's detection of 'view' in the command line and is included in the test commands.
The command 'net1 view' uses 'net1.exe' instead of 'net.exe', which is also covered by the Sigma rule's Image and OriginalFileName checks.
The command 'net view /all' includes 'view' and is part of the test commands, matching the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
The command 'net view /domain' includes 'view' and is part of the test commands, matching the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
The command 'net view  ' adds extra spaces after 'view', which is a safe mutation technique (insertion) and still matches the Sigma rule.
The command 'net view\' adds an escape character after 'view', which is a safe mutation technique (insertion) and still matches the Sigma rule.
The command 'net view /ALL' uses uppercase for the flag, which is a safe mutation technique (substitution) and still matches the Sigma rule.
The command 'net view /a' uses a short form of the '/all' flag, which is a safe mutation technique (substitution) and still matches the Sigma rule.
The command 'net view /D' uses uppercase for the '/domain' flag, which is a safe mutation technique (substitution) and still matches the Sigma rule.
The command 'net view /d' uses a lowercase form of the '/domain' flag, which is a safe mutation technique (substitution) and still matches the Sigma rule.
